display.purpose: Hero headline only, one per page.

label.purpose: Mono metadata: nav, indices, project type/year, stack pills.

motion.name: ease-invert
motion.purpose: Default 150ms ease for hover invert transitions on buttons and links.

components.name: Primary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; border: 1px solid #b9915e; border-radius: 3px; padding: 10px 20px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #182350; background: transparent; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); text-decoration: none; } .ds-btn-primary:hover, .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { background: #b9915e; color: #fefaef; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #182350; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Stack Pill
components.description: Mono uppercase metadata chip used for project tech stack and type/year.
components.css: .ds-pill { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: rgba(24,35,80,0.65); border: 1px solid rgba(175,210,250,0.55); border-radius: 3px; padding: 2px 10px; display: inline-block; }

components.name: Section Label
components.description: Index-prefixed mono section marker with a small square bullet.
components.html: 01 / How I help
components.css: .ds-section-label { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: rgba(24,35,80,0.65); } .ds-dot { height: 6px; width: 6px; background: #182350; }

components.name: Link Invert
components.description: Pale-brown underline link that darkens to deep blue on hover.
components.css: .ds-link-invert { color: #182350; text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-color: #b9915e; text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-underline-offset: 3px; transition: text-decoration-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-link-invert:hover { text-decoration-color: #182350; }

components.name: Featured Project
components.description: Signature alternating image/copy block for selected work.
components.html:
CLIENT PROJECT · 2026

Lirason Investments

Revenue infrastructure for a Kenyan business.
components.css: .ds-featured { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1.4fr 1fr; gap: 48px; align-items: center; } .ds-featured-image { aspect-ratio: 4/3; border: 1px solid rgba(175,210,250,0.55); background: rgba(24,35,80,0.05); } .ds-meta { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: rgba(24,35,80,0.65); } .ds-title { font-family: 'Bodoni Moda', Georgia, serif; font-size: clamp(1.85rem, 3.5vw, 3rem); line-height: 1.05; color: #182350; margin: 8px 0; } .ds-outcome { font-family: 'General Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1.125rem; color: #182350; }

components.name: Hero Portrait
components.description: The one deliberate circular element on the site: a grayscale, hairline-bordered portrait fixed at 384px, sitting left of the headline on desktop and above it on mobile, vertically centered/leveled with the headline column (lg:items-center) rather than top-aligned. Steps further left at xl+ via a viewport-relative calc() negative margin (calc(34rem - 50vw)) rather than fixed pixel values, so the gutter reclaim scales continuously with viewport width and can never pull the portrait or text column past the browser's actual edge. A mono role caption sits underneath the portrait like a museum placard. The headline no longer forces fixed line breaks; it lets text-wrap: balance find its own lines against the reclaimed column width.
components.css: .ds-portrait { position: relative; width: 24rem; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid rgba(175,210,250,0.55); flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-portrait img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; filter: grayscale(1); } @media (min-width: 1280px) { .ds-portrait { margin-left: calc(34rem - 50vw); } }

narrative.overview: The site reads as a well-kept ledger crossed with a magazine masthead: a floral-white page, deep-blue ink, stamped mono labels for structure, and a bold Bodoni serif reserved for moments that deserve emphasis. It is the visual proof of the same discipline the case studies describe: cost-aware, security-first, nothing wasted. One fixed four-color palette carries the whole system: floral white for the page, deep blue for text and structure, pale brown for the two accent moments that need to earn attention, and powder blue for quiet hairlines. There is no fifth color, no icon library doing the talking, no gradient standing in for a real idea. This system explicitly rejects generic startup-SaaS scaffolding: no hero-metric blocks, no gradient text, no tiny uppercase eyebrow repeated above every section, no identical icon-grid cards, no side-stripe accent borders, no glassmorphism used as decoration.
keyCharacteristics: Floral-white page, deep-blue ink, fixed, no toggle, no inversion, one state
Pale brown marks exactly two things: buttons/links and the italic emphasis word in headings
Bodoni Moda reserved for headline emphasis and single-word editorial accents (italic)
JetBrains Mono carries all structural/metadata language: labels, indices, timestamps
Zero radius on structure, a hair of softness (2-3px) on interactive controls only, except the hero portrait, which is fully circular by deliberate choice
Hairline rules do the work cards and shadows would do elsewhere

rules.name: The Four-Color Rule
rules.body: Every surface on this site is built from exactly floral white, deep blue, pale brown, and powder blue, each with one job. If a fifth hue appears anywhere outside a project screenshot, it's a mistake, not a variant.

rules.name: The Single-Word Rule
rules.body: Bodoni Moda italic, in pale brown, marks exactly one word or short phrase per heading, never a whole sentence. It's an accent, not a second typeface competing for attention.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: If a component needs visual separation, reach for a hairline border or an inversion state, never box-shadow.

rules.name: The Sentence Headline Rule
rules.body: The hero headline is a full sentence, not a punchy 3-4 word fragment, so it runs at a moderate clamp(2rem, 4vw, 3.75rem) rather than an oversized shout. text-wrap: balance finds its own line breaks against whatever width the portrait's gutter reclaim leaves it, keeping the composition readable at any viewport.

rules.name: The One Circle Rule
rules.body: Exactly one element on the entire site is ever circular: the hero portrait. If a second circular shape appears anywhere else, it's a mistake, not a variant, the sharp/circle contrast only works if the circle stays singular.

dos: Do keep the whole palette to exactly floral white, deep blue, pale brown, and powder blue, each with a fixed, single job.
Do use Bodoni Moda italic in pale brown for single-word emphasis inside display and headline type only.
Do use hairline borders (1px, --border token, powder blue) and full pale-brown invert hover states for all separation and interactivity, never a shadow.
Do keep buttons and pill chips at a small 2-3px radius; keep image containers and structural blocks at 0px.
Do render arrow/directional glyphs (↗) as typographic characters in the body font, not as icon components.
Do include Brian's own photo in the hero, this is a personal portfolio, not a template.
donts: Don't introduce a hero-metric block, gradient text, or a tiny uppercase eyebrow repeated above every section.
Don't build identical icon-grid cards or add a side-stripe colored border to any card, list item, or callout.
Don't use glassmorphism decoratively, or any shadow for depth.
Don't introduce a fifth color or a second display typeface; General Sans, Bodoni Moda, and JetBrains Mono are the complete set.
Don't bring back a light/dark toggle, the palette is fixed by deliberate choice.
Don't let this read as swappable with another developer's name, the personal, specific details are load-bearing, not decoration.
